scene.description: A photorealistic golden-hour hero exterior of the completed Anna's Farm Site One common-space entry landscape in Lafayette Colorado suburban Front Range. Three-quarter angle, low camera height, looking across the dry-riverbed accent line toward the largest boulder cluster with the stucco wall catching warm back-light. Decomposed granite glows amber in the low sun; boulder faces are rim-lit; flagstone cleft texture is emphasized by raking light. Solar path lights are just beginning to glow a soft amber along the curve.
scene.location: Lafayette, Colorado, Boulder County (40.0004 N, -105.110 W)

materials.type: weathered Colorado moss-rock, ochre with lichen, 18–36 in
buff Lyons sandstone flagstone clusters, 4 tons total
matte-black solar path lights, 2700K, warm glow just activating

lighting.source: low golden-hour sun from camera back-left
lighting.direction: raking across the composition, long shadows stretching camera-right
lighting.practical_lights: solar path lights glowing warm amber, subtle bloom

camera.framing: low 3/4 angle, leading-line composition along the dry riverbed toward the hero boulder cluster, stucco wall upper-right

composition.foreground: rust rip-rap dry-riverbed line cutting diagonal across frame
composition.midground: hero boulder cluster with flagstone seating grouping
composition.background: warm-lit stucco wall and silhouetted cottonwoods

style.reference: Landscape Architecture Magazine feature, Sunset magazine landscape cover
style.film_stock: Portra 400 digital emulation, warm golden-hour bias

include: warm raking highlights on flagstone cleft
natural amber bloom on solar lights
